qa_role_group.name: BMH Content QA - Tech Stack Canary
qa_role_group.description: Private access group for the isolated Tech Stack import canary.

program.title: BMH Employee Training - Canary
program.description: Internal training for serving sellers, operating the pipeline, and growing at BMH Group.

courses.title: BMH Employee Training - Canary
courses.description: Six sequential sections covering the BMH way, seller conversations, operating systems, and performance.

modules.title: Orientation
modules.description: Learn the BMH Group service standard, vocabulary, and operating tools.

lessons.title: Tech Stack and Systems
lessons.description: Understand where lead data, calls, research, practice, time tracking, and team communication belong.

content.html:
What you will learn

Understand where lead data, calls, research, practice, time tracking, and team communication belong.

  • Identify the purpose of each core tool
  • Keep Sandra as the lead source of truth
  • Use Closer Lab for deliberate practice
  • Escalate blocked work through the correct team channel

Learner guide

  • If lead activity is not recorded in Sandra it is not operationally visible
  • Use property tools for research and Sandra for relationship history
  • Complete assigned tasks and next actions on time
  • Ask your manager for the current SOP when a tool workflow changes

Use the current written SOP and ask your manager when a live process differs from this lesson.

content.description: Accessible learner guide for Tech Stack and Systems

cards.front: Which application serves as the central CRM and "single source of truth" for the BMH Group?
According to BMH Group standards, what is the status of a lead or interaction if it is not recorded in Sandra?
Where are team members required to log notes after a conversation with a homeowner?
What action must be taken in Sandra when a deal moves from one stage to the next?
cards.back: The deal stage must be updated.

cards.front: Where are daily tasks and follow-ups assigned by managers located?
How often should a BMH Group team member check their task list in Sandra?
What is the primary purpose of using PropStream within the tech stack?
cards.back: To find motivated sellers by accessing property data.

cards.front: Beyond lead generation, what property-specific information is PropStream used to evaluate?
cards.back: Property details, estimated value, and the owner's likely situation.

quiz.title: Tech Stack and Systems Checkpoint
quiz.description: Each attempt includes every question in the lesson pool in randomized order.

questions.question_text: Which application serves as the central CRM and "single source of truth" for the BMH Group?
questions.explanation: Sandra

options.option_text: DealMachine
Sandra
Deal Sniper
PropStream

questions.question_text: According to BMH Group standards, what is the status of a lead or interaction if it is not recorded in Sandra?
questions.explanation: It does not exist.

options.option_text: It remains unassigned.
It does not exist.
It is visible in Slack.
It counts as completed.

questions.question_text: Where are team members required to log notes after a conversation with a homeowner?
questions.explanation: Sandra

options.option_text: Slack
HubStaff
Sandra
DialPad

questions.question_text: What action must be taken in Sandra when a deal moves from one stage to the next?
questions.explanation: The deal stage must be updated.

options.option_text: The property list must be pulled again.
The offer must be practiced in Closer Lab.
The call recording must be moved to Slack.
The deal stage must be updated.

questions.question_text: Where are daily tasks and follow-ups assigned by managers located?
questions.explanation: Sandra

options.option_text: HubStaff
BMH Institute
Slack
Sandra

questions.question_text: How often should a BMH Group team member check their task list in Sandra?
questions.explanation: Every day.

options.option_text: After each call.
Only when blocked.
Every day.
Once per module.

questions.question_text: What is the primary purpose of using PropStream within the tech stack?
questions.explanation: To find motivated sellers by accessing property data.

options.option_text: To practice objections before speaking with homeowners.
To find motivated sellers by accessing property data.
To track hours and activity for accurate payment.
To coach calls through recorded conversations.

questions.question_text: Beyond lead generation, what property-specific information is PropStream used to evaluate?
questions.explanation: Property details, estimated value, and the owner's likely situation.

options.option_text: Filler words, pacing, and clarity during roleplay sessions.
Call volume, call duration, and recorded coaching sessions.
Quiz results, module progress, and assigned onboarding order.
Property details, estimated value, and the owner's likely situation.

questions.question_text: What is the functional difference between Sandra and PropStream in the lead pipeline?
questions.explanation: PropStream is used to find leads, while Sandra is used to manage the resulting relationships.

options.option_text: PropStream is used to place calls, while Sandra is used to analyze speech during roleplay.
Sandra sources motivated-seller data, while PropStream stores homeowner notes and deal stages.
Sandra is used to track hours, while PropStream is used to store onboarding modules.
PropStream is used to find leads, while Sandra is used to manage the resulting relationships.

questions.question_text: Which tool is used for the "driving for dollars" strategy of sourcing leads?
questions.explanation: DealMachine

options.option_text: Deal Sniper
PropStream
Sandra
DealMachine

questions.question_text: What visual property conditions are flagged using DealMachine in neighborhoods?
questions.explanation: Properties that appear vacant, neglected, or distressed.

options.option_text: Properties with high call volume, long calls, or low clarity.
Properties with active follow-ups, updated stages, or recorded notes.
Properties behind on taxes, in probate, or owned by absentees.
Properties that appear vacant, neglected, or distressed.

questions.question_text: In DealMachine, what can a user do immediately after tagging a distressed property?
questions.explanation: Pull owner information and add them to a marketing campaign.

options.option_text: Update the deal stage and assign a daily follow-up.
Record a homeowner call and review its duration.
Pull owner information and add them to a marketing campaign.
Complete a roleplay and bookmark the related SOP.

questions.question_text: What is the primary role of Deal Sniper in the BMH Group's workflow?
questions.explanation: To generate offers quickly and reliably.

options.option_text: To record homeowner calls for coaching and review.
To store onboarding modules and measure quiz progress.
To track work hours and activity for accurate payment.
To generate offers quickly and reliably.

questions.question_text: Why is speed emphasized when using Deal Sniper to send offers?
questions.explanation: Sending a higher volume of offers increases the probability of transacting a deal.

options.option_text: Sending offers through the system replaces follow-up with the homeowner.
Sending more offers eliminates the need to evaluate which properties are good deals.
Sending offers quickly automatically moves each deal to the next stage in Sandra.
Sending a higher volume of offers increases the probability of transacting a deal.

questions.question_text: How does Deal Sniper assist in property evaluation?
questions.explanation: It helps filter and evaluate properties so the team can move fast on good deals.

options.option_text: It tracks quiz results and module progress so trainees complete lessons in order.
It helps filter and evaluate properties so the team can move fast on good deals.
It supplies distressed-homeowner lists so the team can begin property research.
It records call volume and duration so managers can review dialing activity.

questions.question_text: Which platform is used for all outbound phone calls to homeowners?
questions.explanation: DialPad

options.option_text: DialPad
Closer Lab
HubStaff
Slack

questions.question_text: What is the primary reason BMH Group records homeowner calls in DialPad?
questions.explanation: To provide coaching and help team members improve their skills.

options.option_text: To replace the relationship notes team members are required to enter in Sandra.
To provide coaching and help team members improve their skills.
To calculate the hours and activity used to determine accurate payment.
To identify code violations and absentee owners for property research.

questions.question_text: What call metrics are visible to managers through the DialPad system?
questions.explanation: Call volume and call duration.

options.option_text: Property value and owner situation.
Call volume and call duration.
Quiz results and progress.
Filler words and pacing.

questions.question_text: What is Closer Lab?
questions.explanation: An AI-driven speech coaching tool used for training and roleplay.

options.option_text: A property data tool used to find and evaluate motivated-seller leads.
A time and activity tracking tool used to support accurate payment.
A central CRM used to manage lead relationships and deal stages.
An AI-driven speech coaching tool used for training and roleplay.

questions.question_text: What specific exercise should be completed in Closer Lab before speaking with a real homeowner?
questions.explanation: Objection role plays and call simulations.

options.option_text: Property research and estimated-value reviews.
Objection role plays and call simulations.
SOP bookmarking and onboarding-module quizzes.
Call-volume checks and HubStaff activity reviews.

questions.question_text: If a task is blocked or a team member needs help, where should they flag it to their manager?
questions.explanation: Slack

options.option_text: Slack
Sandra
DialPad
HubStaff

questions.question_text: Which software is used at BMH Group for time tracking and activity monitoring?
questions.explanation: HubStaff

options.option_text: Closer Lab
DialPad
HubStaff
Slack

questions.question_text: What is the mandatory clock-in/clock-out protocol for HubStaff?
questions.explanation: Clock in when starting work and clock out when stopping.

options.option_text: Clock in after checking Sandra and clock out after the final homeowner call.
Clock in when a manager posts in Slack and clock out after reading updates.
Clock in when starting work and clock out when stopping.
Clock in for Closer Lab practice and clock out before completing daily tasks.

questions.question_text: What is the primary internal communication tool used by BMH Group?
questions.explanation: Slack

options.option_text: Google Docs
Email
Sandra
Slack

questions.question_text: Which standard communication method does BMH Group explicitly avoid for internal operations?
questions.explanation: Email

options.option_text: Slack
Email
DialPad
Sandra

questions.question_text: What are the two requirements for staying connected to the business via Slack?
questions.explanation: Keeping notifications on and staying active to see wins and updates.

options.option_text: Completing modules in order and tracking quiz results.
Clocking in for work and clocking out when stopping.
Keeping notifications on and staying active to see wins and updates.
Logging every lead and updating each deal stage.

questions.question_text: What is the BMH Institute?
questions.explanation: The company's training platform containing all onboarding modules.

options.option_text: The property research platform containing motivated-seller data.
The speech coaching platform containing objection roleplays and call simulations.
The company's training platform containing all onboarding modules.
The central CRM containing all homeowner notes and deal stages.

questions.question_text: How should a trainee approach the modules within the BMH Institute?
questions.explanation: Work through the modules in order, as each builds on the previous one.

options.option_text: Work through the modules based on the order of daily tasks in Sandra.
Work through the modules in order, as each builds on the previous one.
Complete the quizzes first, then return to the onboarding modules.
Use only modules tied to a current homeowner conversation or blocked task.

questions.question_text: Cloze: All company SOPs, scripts, and templates are stored in _____.
questions.explanation: Google Docs (or Google Drive)

options.option_text: Sandra notes (or task lists)
Google Docs (or Google Drive)
BMH Institute modules (or quizzes)
Slack updates (or announcements)

questions.question_text: What is the recommended action for team members regarding relevant Google Drive documents?
questions.explanation: Bookmark the documents most relevant to their daily work.

options.option_text: Use the documents to replace assigned modules in the BMH Institute.
Share every document as a team announcement in Slack.
Enter the documents as homeowner notes in Sandra.
Bookmark the documents most relevant to their daily work.

questions.question_text: What is the consequence of skipping steps or working around the established tech stack?
questions.explanation: Deals will fall through the cracks and get lost.

options.option_text: Quiz results will stop showing training module progress.
Call recordings will stop showing volume and duration.
Property research will stop showing value and owner details.
Deals will fall through the cracks and get lost.

questions.question_text: Term: Driving for Dollars
questions.explanation: Definition: Neighborhood sourcing by flagging vacant or neglected properties using DealMachine.

options.option_text: Definition: Neighborhood sourcing by flagging vacant or neglected properties using DealMachine.
Definition: Property research using data on probate, taxes, code violations, or absentee owners.
Definition: Homeowner call recording and activity review using DialPad.
Definition: Rapid property filtering and offer generation using Deal Sniper.

questions.question_text: Why does BMH Group track call volume and duration in DialPad?
questions.explanation: To monitor activity and ensure team members are making their dials.

options.option_text: To calculate hours worked and determine accurate payment through HubStaff.
To measure filler words, pacing, and clarity during Closer Lab roleplay.
To estimate property values and identify each owner's likely situation.
To monitor activity and ensure team members are making their dials.

questions.question_text: What is the benefit of practicing in Closer Lab before live calls?
questions.explanation: It allows for practice without the pressure of a live call with a homeowner.

options.option_text: It replaces the requirement to record live homeowner activity in Sandra.
It tracks work hours and activity instead of requiring HubStaff.
It allows for practice without the pressure of a live call with a homeowner.
It generates property offers without filtering or evaluating good deals.

questions.question_text: What kind of lists does a manager provide to a team member that originate from PropStream?
questions.explanation: Lists of distressed homeowners.

options.option_text: Lists of recorded homeowner calls.
Lists of distressed homeowners.
Lists of assigned training modules.
Lists of completed follow-ups.

questions.question_text: How does Deal Sniper help team members handle motivated homeowners effectively?
questions.explanation: It enables them to make confident offers quickly without wasting time.

options.option_text: It records their homeowner conversations so managers can coach their call skills.
It gives them distressed-homeowner lists before the team evaluates any properties.
It enables them to make confident offers quickly without wasting time.
It assigns their daily tasks and stores the relationship history for each lead.

questions.question_text: What is tracked within the BMH Institute to ensure trainees understand the material?
questions.explanation: Quiz results and module progress.

options.option_text: Call volume and call duration.
Hours worked and activity level.
Property value and owner situation.
Quiz results and module progress.

questions.question_text: Term: HubStaff
questions.explanation: Definition: A tool that tracks hours and activity to ensure accurate payment for work put in.

options.option_text: Definition: A call platform that records volume and duration for manager review.
Definition: A CRM that stores lead relationships, notes, tasks, and deal stages.
Definition: A tool that tracks hours and activity to ensure accurate payment for work put in.
Definition: A training platform that stores onboarding modules, quiz results, and progress.

questions.question_text: Where are company wins, updates, and team announcements shared?
questions.explanation: Slack

options.option_text: DialPad
Sandra
Google Docs
Slack

questions.explanation: Dropping the lead directly into a marketing campaign.

options.option_text: Completing an objection roleplay in Closer Lab.
Reviewing the call duration in DialPad.
Updating the deal stage in Sandra.
Dropping the lead directly into a marketing campaign.

questions.question_text: What are the two components of the 'Standard' regarding data in Sandra?
questions.explanation: Log everything and if it's not logged, it doesn't exist.

options.option_text: Log everything and if it's not logged, it doesn't exist.
Record every call and use duration as the deal stage.
Pull property data and send every homeowner an offer.
Complete every module and log quiz results in Slack.

questions.question_text: In the BMH tech stack, which tool is specifically used for filtering properties to find good deals fast?
questions.explanation: Deal Sniper

options.option_text: Deal Sniper
Sandra
PropStream
DealMachine

questions.question_text: Why is organization considered essential in the real estate business according to the text?
questions.explanation: It ensures that nothing is dropped and deals are handled efficiently.

options.option_text: It ensures that nothing is dropped and deals are handled efficiently.
It allows property research to replace relationship management in Sandra.
It allows training modules to replace assigned daily tasks.
It allows call recordings to replace live homeowner follow-up.

questions.question_text: Cloze: When you get a new lead, the first step is to enter it into _____.
questions.explanation: Sandra

options.option_text: Deal Sniper
DealMachine
PropStream
Sandra
